challenges.title: Array Basics
challenges.description:
Write a C++ program that declares an array of 5 integers, initializes it with values using an initializer list, and uses a for loop to print each element. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.

Your program must:
- Declare an int array of size 5
- Initialize the array with values using an initializer list { }
- Use a for loop to iterate through the array
- Display each element with cout
challenges.test_code:
assert 'int' in cpp and '[' in cpp and ']' in cpp, 'Should declare an int array with square bracket notation'
assert '{' in cpp and '}' in cpp, 'Should use an initializer list with curly braces'
assert 'for' in cpp, 'Should use a for loop to iterate through the array'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Should display each element with cout'
assert '5' in cpp, 'Array should have 5 elements'
hints: Access elements with an index: numbers[i] inside a for loop

challenges.title: Array Functions and Vectors
challenges.description:
Write a C++ program that defines a function taking an int array and its size as parameters, which calculates and returns the sum of all elements. Also declare a vector, add elements with push_back, and display them. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.

Your program must:
- Include the vector header
- Define a function that accepts an array parameter and a size parameter
- The function should return the sum of the array elements
- Declare a vector and use push_back to add elements
- Display results with cout
challenges.test_code:
assert '#include <vector>' in cpp, 'Should include the vector header'
assert 'vector' in cpp, 'Should declare a vector'
assert 'push_back' in cpp, 'Should use push_back to add elements to the vector'
assert 'int ' in cpp and '[]' in cpp, 'Should have a function that takes an array parameter'
assert 'return' in cpp, 'The function should return the sum'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Should display results with cout'
assert cpp.count('for') >= 1, 'Should use a loop to process array or vector elements'
hints: Function signature: int sumArray(int arr[], int size)
Declare a vector: vector<int> nums; then nums.push_back(10);
